classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

ccel.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian
ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

verses.text: To all these heretics is added one Tatian, a brother-heretic.
This man was Justin Martyr’s disciple.
After Justin’s death he began to cherish different opinions from his.
For he wholly savours of Valentinus; adding this, that Adam cannot even attain salvation: as if, when the branches become salvable, the root were not!
Other heretics swell the list who are called Cataphrygians, but their teaching is not uniform.
For there are (of them) some who are called Cataproclans; there are others who are termed Catæschinetans.
These have a blasphemy common, and a blasphemy not common, but peculiar and special.

The common blasphemy lies in their saying that the Holy Spirit was in the apostles indeed, the Paraclete was not; and in their saying that the Paraclete has spoken in Montanus more things than Christ brought forward into (the compass of) the Gospel, and not merely more, but likewise better and greater.
But the particular one they who follow Æschines have; this, namely, whereby they add this, that they affirm Christ to be Himself Son and Father.
